\newpage

# Recipe One

 \normalsize

![Pad Thai](website/images/PadThai.jpg){width=50%}

## Ingredients

- 14 oz Thai stir fry noodles
- 2 limes
- 2 tablespoons tamari (or regular soy sauce)
- 1/4 cup sweet and sour sauce
- 2 bell peppers
- 4 garlic cloves
- 1 package extra-firm tofu
- 1/4 cup roasted peanuts
- 2 eggs


## Instructions

1. Boil Thai noodles according to package instructions. Rince under cold water and coat with oil.
1. Drain tofu, cut into bite-sized pieces, and season with salt and pepper.
1. Thinly slice peppers, mince garlic, chop peanuts.
1. Squeeze 2 tablespoons lime juice into a small bowl. Mix in tamari and sweet and sour sauce.
1. Heat oil in a frying pan over medium heat. Cook tofu until golden brown (5-10 minutes). Move to a paper towel lined plate.
1. In the same pan, add oil and cook bell peppers until tender. Add to the plate with the tofu.
1. In the same pan, add oil and garlic. Cook 1-2 minutes until browned.
1. Add noodles and sauce and cook about 3-5 minutes, until noodles absorb some sauce.
1. Move noodles to one side of the pan. Add a little oil and two eggs. Scramble the eggs until almost set, then stir them into the noodles.
1. Mix in tofu, peppers, and peanuts.
1. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Serve with lime wedges.

 \normalsize

\newpage

# Recipe Two

 \normalsize

![Bibimbap (Serves 4)](website/images/Bibimbap.jpg){width=50%}

## Ingredients

- 1 cup rice
- 8 oz mushrooms
- 3 garlic cloves
- 8 oz sugar snap peas
- 2 tsp gochujang
- 2 tablespoon mayonnaise
- 3 tsp sesame oil
- 5 oz spinach
- 4 eggs
- 1 cup kimchi


## Instructions

1. Cook 1/2 cup rice according to package instructions.
1. Thinly slice mushrooms, mince garlic. Trim stem ends and strings from snap peas.
1. Combine garlic, gochujang, 1 tsp sesame oil, and mayonnaise. Mix in water 1 tsp at a time until sauce can be drizzled.
1. Cook mushrooms in a bit of oil until lightly browned.
1. Add sugar snap peas and cook for 3-4 minutes.
1. Add spinach and cook until wilted.
1. Add 2 tsp sesame oil and a pinch of salt and pepper.
1. Fry 4 eggs.
1. Divide rice, kimchi, stirfried vegetables, and eggs between bowls. Drizzle with gochujang sauce.

 \normalsize

\newpage

# Recipe Three


 \normalsize

![BLT Salad (Serves 4)](website/images/BLTSalad.jpg){width=50%}

## Ingredients

- 1 pound tri-color spiral pasta
- 16-oz pre-cut crisp lettuce (e.g., romaine, baby butter leaf)
- 12 ounces grape tomatoes
- 1.5 cups shredded cheddar cheese
- 1 pound of thick cut bacon
- 1 jar of ranch dressing


## Instructions

1. Prepare pasta according to package instructions.
1. Cook bacon to be crispy.
1. Refrigerate pasta and bacon until cold.
1. Halve tomatoes and dice cooked bacon.
1. Add lettuce, tomato, cheese, bacon, and dressing to taste in a bowl and toss.

 \normalsize

\newpage

# Breakfast Recipe


 \normalsize

![Overnight Oats](website/images/OvernightOats.jpg){width=50%}

## Ingredients

- 1/4 tsp ground cinnamon
- 1/4 tsp ginger paste
- 2 tablespoons pine nuts
- 1 cups rolled oats
- 1 tablespoons maple syrup
- 4 teaspoons fig butter
- 1 plums
- 1 cups milk


## Instructions

1. Combine milk, cinnamon, and ginger, with a pinch of salt in a 1-pint wide mouthed mason jar.
1. Whisk oats and maple syrup. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
1. Thinly slice plums.
1. Split between two 1-pint wide mouthed mason jars. Top with fig butter, plums and pine nuts.

 \normalsize
